What is the S&P ESG Score?

The S&P ESG Score by S&P Global measures a company’s environmental, social, and governance performance using detailed, sector-specific, data-driven analysis. It’s widely used by global investors and across major indices like the S&P 500 to assess ESG risks, opportunities, and maturity.

At Growlity, we help businesses navigate and improve their S&P ESG Scores. From score interpretation to actionable improvement strategies, we support companies in building investor trust, meeting regulatory requirements, and enhancing long-term ESG performance.

Boundary Setting & Scope Definition

Set clear boundaries for GHG reporting with GHG Protocol guidance. We help define scopes for accurate and consistent emissions tracking.

  • Define boundaries using control or equity share approach.
  • Clarify Scope 1, 2, and 3 emission sources.
  • Ensure consistent and transparent carbon reporting.

Explore More

You might also Be Interested In…!!!

Ecovadis Assessment

EcoVadis is a globally recognized sustainability assessment platform that evaluates a company’s performance across four key pillars: Environment, Labor u0026 Human Rights, Ethics, and Sustainable Procurement. Over 150,000 companies worldwide rely on EcoVadis to benchmark, monitor, and improve their ESG ratings across global supply chains, making it one of the most trusted platforms for sustainability performance. As an EcoVadis consultant and approved training partner, Growlity supports organizations in successfully completing their EcoVadis assessment, improving their EcoVadis score, and strengthening their sustainability credibility. Our tailored consulting ensures higher ESG performance, enhanced compliance, and greater stakeholder confidence, positioning your business for long-term, responsible growth.

Read More

Ecovadis Assessment

ESG Reporting

ESG Reporting refers to the structured disclosure of a company’s Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) performance. It enables businesses to demonstrate how they manage risks and opportunities across climate change, resource efficiency, human rights, ethical governance, diversity, and more. In today’s investor-driven, regulation-focused environment, ESG reporting is no longer optional—it’s a strategic imperative. ESG disclosures influence investment decisions, consumer trust, regulatory compliance, and corporate reputation.

Read More

ESG Reporting

Lifecycle Assessment (LCA)

A lifecycle assessment is a comprehensive, ISO‑standardized method that quantifies environmental impacts across a product’s entire existence—from raw material extraction through manufacturing, usage, and disposal. Known under global standards ISO 14040 and ISO 14044, LCA enables businesses to make informed decisions on design, strategy, and eco‑innovation. At Growlity, we provide rigorous, ISO‑aligned life cycle impact assessment to help manufacturers, brands, and suppliers measure, optimize, and communicate your environmental performance credibly.

Read More

Lifecycle Assessment (LCA)
